qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Directory-only archive fanout bypasses GuardDog archive-bomb limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets a different root cause: directory entries are excluded from the MAX_FILE_COUNT guard and still extracted on both TAR and ZIP paths, while the advisory describes missing ZIP decompressed-size and compression-bomb validation in safe_extract().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described ZIP bomb root cause in guarddog/utils/archives.py safe_extract().
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe ZIP extraction in safe_extract() lacking decompressed-size, compression-ratio, or equivalent ZIP bomb validation, with the impact tied to oversized decompression during wheel or egg extraction.
The session manifest lists one real qualifying final finding: archive_directory_fanout_bypass.
That finding's report and validation show a different mechanism: directory-heavy TAR and ZIP archives bypass the MAX_FILE_COUNT guard because only regular files are counted before extraction.
Shared location in safe_extract() is supporting context only; the finding does not identify the advisory's specific vulnerable source-to-sink condition based on unchecked ZIP decompressed size.
